decisions.rationale: This is the latest WP-01 document in the batch and explicitly hardens the Context Graph for execution determinism, snapshot semantics, policy-checked queries, and no-silent-mutation rules.
This document includes grammar, AST, type system, execution semantics, conflict resolution, policy packs, and security guarantees. It supersedes conceptual NPL guideline uploads.
This is the cleanest uploaded strategic blueprint for the Noetfield Developer OS orchestration and policy layer.
This document is the clearest work-package dependency and roadmap registry for remaining Developer OS components.
This is the strongest uploaded Security Agent source, but it should be formalized into WP-05 Agent Behavior Monitoring and Security Agent specs before becoming executable.

Perplexity guidelines provide the strongest architecture-level framing around unified state, trust infrastructure, persistent memory, and policy-driven orchestration. Grok is retained as practical tooling reference.
Useful operator enablement reference for Cursor workflows, but not a platform governance source-of-truth.
POSA launch plan is a GTM reference for a separate product concept and should not affect Noetfield runtime governance unless explicitly scoped later.
POSA v3.0 is the highest-level source-of-truth for the personal operating system agent architecture. POSA v3.1 revenue documents are subsystem sources under this root specification.
POSA v3.1 is the newest and most complete autonomous revenue subsystem specification and supersedes earlier revenue automation/playbook documents for revenue architecture.
This is the strongest uploaded source for POSA digital twin and memory semantics, including memory governance and event-log rules.
Defines POSA multi-tenant SaaS evolution, but should remain separate from single-user POSA v3.0 source-of-truth until product line is explicitly selected.
Defines deployable POSA Docker/cloud architecture; useful if POSA becomes implemented product line.
Most concrete POSA MVP execution plan for a first working Telegram-controlled revenue loop.
Separate Shopify product blueprint; does not govern POSA or Noetfield unless later adopted as a product module.
Provides theoretical framing for constraint architecture, co-cognitive alignment, and latent interaction. Useful for future prompt/intent architecture but not executable runtime authority.
PAAS v1, PAES v1, and POSA v2 are historical predecessors. POSA v3.0 remains the active POSA root SOT.
The full technical whitepaper is the normative DCEN specification. Batch 004 module architecture remains a supporting module reference.
The mathematical model document supersedes the earlier adaptive-supply tokenomics upload with staking yield equilibrium and stress scenarios.
Monorepo structure defines where contracts, cosmos modules, agent-runtime, and infra live when AIE implementation is scoped.
AIIS is enterprise agent infrastructure positioning, distinct from Noetfield governance runtime and AIE tokenized agent economy.
Manifesto encodes constraint-driven agent design doctrine that aligns with Noetfield governance posture without being executable policy.
Book proposal is a standalone publishing product about context engineering, not platform source-of-truth.
